text:
Once upon a time in the little town of Woodberry, there lived Mr. Chalkbot. Mr. Chalkbot was not actually a human; he was a small, blackboard with chalk written all over him. He lived at the town hall, where everyone could see him.

Mr. Chalkbot was quite lonely. Despite his chalky exterior, he wished for companionship. One evening, he laid out chalk dust to form a pattern in the sand outside the hall. That night, a mysterious owl landed on the pattern, and somehow, it understood Mr. Chalkbot. That very night, Mr. Chalkbot began to write in the owl's plumage: "Would you like to hang out?"

And so, a friendship began. Each morning, Mr. Chalkbot would mark the days with different designs. Each night, the owl would return and sketch new patterns. Their routine amazed the townsfolk who saw the owl conversing with Mr. Chalkbot
